---
title: "Witness Tree Project: Comprehensive Analysis"
author: "Conor Walsh"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_notebook:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
---

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 0: SETUP, LIBRARIES, PATHS, AND HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================
# This section loads all required packages, sets up file paths,
# defines helper functions for the entire analysis.
# ============================================================

# ---------------------------
# 0.1 Install missing packages (if any)
# ---------------------------
required_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse", "ggplot2", "knitr", "broom", "purrr", "corrplot",
  "ggpubr", "ggthemes", "patchwork", "lme4", "lmerTest", "emmeans",
  "ggrepel", "readxl", "rstatix", "grid", "gridExtra", "cowplot",
  "viridis", "scales", "Hmisc", "psych", "performance", "see"
)

for (pkg in required_packages) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg)
  }
}

# ---------------------------
# 0.2 Load libraries
# ---------------------------
library(tidyverse)   # data manipulation and visualization
library(ggplot2)     # advanced plotting
library(knitr)       # nice tables
library(broom)       # tidy model outputs
library(purrr)       # functional programming
library(corrplot)    # correlation matrices
library(ggpubr)      # publication-ready plots
library(ggthemes)    # extra themes
library(patchwork)   # combine plots
library(lme4)        # mixed models
library(lmerTest)    # p-values for mixed models
library(emmeans)     # estimated marginal means
library(ggrepel)     # repel text labels
library(readxl)      # read Excel files
library(rstatix)     # pipe-friendly statistics
library(grid)        # graphical elements
library(gridExtra)   # arrange grid plots
library(cowplot)     # complex plot arrangements
library(viridis)     # colour-blind friendly palettes
library(scales)      # label formatting
library(Hmisc)       # correlation with p-values
library(psych)       # descriptive statistics
library(performance) # model diagnostics
library(see)         # model visualization

# ---------------------------
# 0.3 Set global options
# ---------------------------
options(scipen = 999)          # avoid scientific notation
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
set.seed(42)                   # reproducibility
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))  # default theme

# ---------------------------
# 0.4 Define paths
# ---------------------------
# Assuming the working directory is the project root
data_dir   <- "Data"
clean_dir  <- file.path(data_dir, "clean_data")
outputs_dir <- file.path(data_dir, "outputs")
figures_dir <- outputs_dir
tables_dir  <- outputs_dir

# Create output directories if they don't exist
dir.create(outputs_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(outputs_dir, "figures"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(outputs_dir, "tables"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Paths to the two clean Excel files
nbg_file <- file.path(clean_dir, "NBGall.xlsx")
tcdbg_file <- file.path(clean_dir, "masters_all_years.xlsx")

# Check if files exist
if (!file.exists(nbg_file)) stop("NBG file not found: ", nbg_file)
if (!file.exists(tcdbg_file)) stop("TCDBG file not found: ", tcdbg_file)

cat("✅ Paths configured successfully.\n")
cat("  NBG file:", nbg_file, "\n")
cat("  TCDBG file:", tcdbg_file, "\n")

# ---------------------------
# 0.5 Helper functions
# ---------------------------

# Standard error function
se <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) <= 1) return(NA_real_)
  sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
}

# Coefficient of variation
cv <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) <= 1) return(NA_real_)
  m <- mean(x)
  if (is.na(m) || m == 0) return(NA_real_)
  sd(x) / m
}

# Pretty species names for plotting (with italics)
pretty_species <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
    str_to_sentence() %>%
    str_replace_all(" X ", " × ") %>%
    str_replace_all(" Subsp ", " subsp. ") %>%
    str_replace_all(" Var ", " var. ")
}

# Clean species names for joining (lowercase, underscores)
clean_species_name <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_to_lower() %>%
    str_replace_all(" ", "_") %>%
    str_replace_all("×", "x") %>%
    str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9_]", "") %>%
    str_replace_all("_+", "_") %>%
    str_replace_all("_$", "")
}

# Significance stars
sig_stars <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "",
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01 ~ "**",
    p < 0.05 ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ "ns"
  )
}

# Format p-values for tables
format_p <- function(p, digits = 3) {
  ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", round(p, digits))
}

# Colour palettes
pal_site <- c("TCDBG" = "#1F78B4", "NBG" = "#E6550D")
pal_pm <- c("PM10" = "#2C6E49", "PM2.5" = "#C62828")
pal_evergreen <- c("Deciduous" = "#90A4AE", "Evergreen" = "#2E7D32")
pal_trichomes <- c("No" = "#90A4AE", "Yes" = "#6A1B9A")
pal_texture <- c("smooth" = "#A6CEE3", "medium" = "#1F78B4", "rough" = "#E31A23")

# Custom theme for publication-ready plots
theme_wtp <- function(base_size = 12) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 2, hjust = 0),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_size - 1, colour = "grey40"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(colour = "black"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey90", linewidth = 0.3),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = base_size - 2, colour = "grey50")
    )
}

# Save plot helper
save_plot_wtp <- function(plot_obj, filename, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300) {
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(figures_dir, filename),
    plot = plot_obj,
    width = width,
    height = height,
    dpi = dpi,
    bg = "white"
  )
  cat("  Saved:", filename, "\n")
}

cat("\n✅ Helper functions loaded.\n")
cat("Ready for Section 1: Data Loading and Aggregation.\n")
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 1: LOAD AND AGGREGATE DATA
# ============================================================
# This section loads the two clean Excel files, inspects their structure,
# and aggregates particle-level data to sample-level.
# Output: nbg_data and tcdbg_data (sample-level data frames)
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                     SECTION 1: LOAD AND AGGREGATE DATA                         ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 1.1 Load NBG data
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.1 Loading NBG data ---\n")

nbg_raw <- read_excel(nbg_file)
cat("  Rows:", nrow(nbg_raw), "\n")
cat("  Columns:", ncol(nbg_raw), "\n")
cat("  Column names:\n")
print(names(nbg_raw))

# Quick glimpse of first rows
cat("\n  First 5 rows (selected columns):\n")
print(nbg_raw %>% 
        select(any_of(c("species_name", "Year", "class", "areaμm", "source_file"))) %>% 
        head(5))

# ---------------------------
# 1.2 Load TCDBG data
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.2 Loading TCDBG data ---\n")

tcdbg_raw <- read_excel(tcdbg_file)
cat("  Rows:", nrow(tcdbg_raw), "\n")
cat("  Columns:", ncol(tcdbg_raw), "\n")
cat("  Column names:\n")
print(names(tcdbg_raw))

cat("\n  First 5 rows (selected columns):\n")
print(tcdbg_raw %>% 
        select(any_of(c("species_name", "Year", "Class", "Areaμm", "source_file"))) %>% 
        head(5))



# DIAGNOSTIC: check grain of raw data by year
cat("\nTCDBG raw rows per species per year (should be >> 1 if particle-level):\n")
tcdbg_raw %>%
  group_by(Year, species_name) %>%
  summarise(n_rows = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(median_rows_per_species = median(n_rows),
            min_rows = min(n_rows),
            max_rows = max(n_rows)) %>%
  print()

# ---------------------------
# 1.3 Standardise column names
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.3 Standardising column names ---\n")

# For NBG: rename key columns to a consistent standard
nbg_std <- nbg_raw %>%
  rename(
    species = species_name,
    year = Year,
    pm_class = class,
    area_um2 = areaμm,
    ecd_um = ecdμm,
    perimeter_um = perimeterμm,
    sample_id = source_file
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    site = "NBG",
    year = as.integer(year)
  )

# For TCDBG: rename key columns
tcdbg_std <- tcdbg_raw %>%
  rename(
    species = species_name,
    year = Year,
    pm_class = Class,
    area_um2 = Areaμm,
    ecd_um = ECDμm,
    perimeter_um = Perimeterμm,
    sample_id = Code           # was: source_file
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    site = "TCDBG",
    year = as.integer(year)
  )

cat("✅ Column names standardised.\n")

# ---------------------------
# 1.4 Filter valid PM classes (PM10, PM2.5)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.4 Filtering valid PM classes ---\n")

standardise_pm_class <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    str_detect(tolower(x), "pm10") ~ "PM10",
    str_detect(tolower(x), "pm2\\.5") ~ "PM2.5",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

nbg_std <- nbg_std %>%
  mutate(pm_type = standardise_pm_class(pm_class)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pm_type), !is.na(area_um2), area_um2 > 0)

tcdbg_std <- tcdbg_std %>%
  mutate(pm_type = standardise_pm_class(pm_class)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pm_type), !is.na(area_um2), area_um2 > 0)

cat("  NBG valid particles:", nrow(nbg_std), "\n")
cat("  TCDBG valid particles:", nrow(tcdbg_std), "\n")

# ---------------------------
# 1.5 Aggregate to sample-level
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.5 Aggregating to sample-level ---\n")

# NBG: group by sample_id, year, pm_type, species, and trait columns (constant per species)
nbg_sample <- nbg_std %>%
  group_by(sample_id, year, pm_type, species, site) %>%
  summarise(
    n_particles = n(),
    mean_particle_area_um2 = mean(area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_particle_area_um2 = median(area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_particle_area_um2 = sd(area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_particle_area_um2 = se(area_um2),
    # Take first trait values (they are constant per species within the dataset)
    evergreen = first(evergreen),
    trichomes = first(trichomes),
    surface_texture = first(surface_texture),
    leaf_size_class = first(leaf_size_class),
    canopy_density = first(canopy_density),
    mean_angle = first(mean_angle),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# TCDBG: same grouping
tcdbg_sample <- tcdbg_std %>%
  group_by(sample_id, year, pm_type, species, site) %>%
  summarise(
    n_particles = n(),
    mean_particle_area_um2 = mean(area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_particle_area_um2 = median(area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_particle_area_um2 = sd(area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_particle_area_um2 = se(area_um2),
    evergreen = first(evergreen),
    trichomes = first(trichomes),
    surface_texture = first(surface_texture),
    leaf_size_class = first(leaf_size_class),
    canopy_density = first(canopy_density),
    mean_angle = first(mean_angle),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("  NBG samples:", nrow(nbg_sample), "\n")
cat("  TCDBG samples:", nrow(tcdbg_sample), "\n")

# ---------------------------
# 1.6 Create combined dataset for comparative analyses (optional)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.6 Creating combined dataset (optional) ---\n")

combined_sample <- bind_rows(nbg_sample, tcdbg_sample) %>%
  mutate(
    log_particles = log(n_particles + 1),
    log_particle_size = log(mean_particle_area_um2 + 1),
    pm_type = factor(pm_type, levels = c("PM10", "PM2.5")),
    site = factor(site, levels = c("TCDBG", "NBG"))
  )

cat("  Combined samples:", nrow(combined_sample), "\n")
cat("  Years:", paste(sort(unique(combined_sample$year)), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("  Species:", n_distinct(combined_sample$species), "\n")

# ---------------------------
# 1.7 Quick summary statistics
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.7 Summary statistics ---\n")

cat("\nNBG particle counts per sample (by PM type):\n")
nbg_sample %>%
  group_by(pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_n = round(mean(n_particles), 1),
    sd_n = round(sd(n_particles), 1),
    median_n = median(n_particles),
    range_n = paste(min(n_particles), "-", max(n_particles))
  ) %>%
  print()

cat("\nTCDBG particle counts per sample (by PM type):\n")
tcdbg_sample %>%
  group_by(pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_n = round(mean(n_particles), 1),
    sd_n = round(sd(n_particles), 1),
    median_n = median(n_particles),
    range_n = paste(min(n_particles), "-", max(n_particles))
  ) %>%
  print()

cat("\nNBG particle sizes (µm²) per sample (by PM type):\n")
nbg_sample %>%
  group_by(pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_size = round(mean(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    sd_size = round(sd(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    median_size = round(median(mean_particle_area_um2), 2)
  ) %>%
  print()

cat("\nTCDBG particle sizes (µm²) per sample (by PM type):\n")
tcdbg_sample %>%
  group_by(pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_size = round(mean(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    sd_size = round(sd(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    median_size = round(median(mean_particle_area_um2), 2)
  ) %>%
  print()

# ---------------------------
# 1.8 Save aggregated data for later use
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 1.8 Saving aggregated data ---\n")

write_csv(nbg_sample, file.path(tables_dir, "nbg_sample_level.csv"))
write_csv(tcdbg_sample, file.path(tables_dir, "tcdbg_sample_level.csv"))
write_csv(combined_sample, file.path(tables_dir, "combined_sample_level.csv"))

cat("✅ Saved sample-level data to tables directory.\n")

cat("\n✅ Section 1 complete. Data loaded and aggregated.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 2: DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS AND DATA OVERVIEW
# ============================================================
# This section provides comprehensive summaries of the sample-level data,
# including distributions, summary tables, and initial visualisations.
# We analyse NBG and TCDBG separately, then compare.
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n              SECTION 2: DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS AND DATA OVERVIEW               ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 2.1 Overall summary tables
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.1 Overall summary by site and PM type ---\n")

# Function to create summary table
summary_table <- function(df, site_name) {
  df %>%
    group_by(pm_type) %>%
    summarise(
      n_samples = n(),
      n_species = n_distinct(species),
      n_years = n_distinct(year),
      years = paste(sort(unique(year)), collapse = ", "),
      # Particle count metrics
      mean_particles = round(mean(n_particles), 1),
      sd_particles = round(sd(n_particles), 1),
      median_particles = median(n_particles),
      min_particles = min(n_particles),
      max_particles = max(n_particles),
      # Particle size metrics
      mean_size = round(mean(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
      sd_size = round(sd(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
      median_size = round(median(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
      min_size = round(min(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
      max_size = round(max(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(site = site_name) %>%
    select(site, everything())
}

nbg_summary <- summary_table(nbg_sample, "NBG")
tcdbg_summary <- summary_table(tcdbg_sample, "TCDBG")
combined_summary <- bind_rows(nbg_summary, tcdbg_summary)

cat("\n=== Summary by site and PM type ===\n")
print(combined_summary)

# Save table
write_csv(combined_summary, file.path(tables_dir, "summary_by_site_pm.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 2.2 Yearly summary
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.2 Yearly summary ---\n")

yearly_summary <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, year, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    mean_particles = round(mean(n_particles), 1),
    se_particles = round(se(n_particles), 2),
    mean_size = round(mean(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    se_size = round(se(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nYearly summary:\n")
print(yearly_summary)

write_csv(yearly_summary, file.path(tables_dir, "yearly_summary.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 2.3 Species summary (overall)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.3 Species summary (across all years) ---\n")

species_summary <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, species, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    mean_particles = round(mean(n_particles), 1),
    sd_particles = round(sd(n_particles), 1),
    mean_size = round(mean(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    sd_size = round(sd(mean_particle_area_um2), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(site, pm_type, desc(mean_particles))

cat("\nTop 5 species by particle count (NBG, PM10):\n")
species_summary %>% filter(site == "NBG", pm_type == "PM10") %>% head(5) %>% print()

cat("\nTop 5 species by particle count (TCDBG, PM10):\n")
species_summary %>% filter(site == "TCDBG", pm_type == "PM10") %>% head(5) %>% print()

write_csv(species_summary, file.path(tables_dir, "species_summary.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 2.4 Distribution plots
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.4 Generating distribution plots ---\n")

# Plot A: Particle count distribution (log scale)
p_count_dist <- combined_sample %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_particles, fill = site)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, bins = 30, position = "identity") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = label_comma()) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of particle counts per sample",
    x = "Number of particles per sample (log scale)",
    y = "Count of samples",
    fill = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_count_dist, "S01_particle_count_distribution.png", width = 10, height = 6)

# Plot B: Particle size distribution
p_size_dist <- combined_sample %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_particle_area_um2, fill = site)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, bins = 30, position = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of mean particle size per sample",
    x = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*")"),
    y = "Count of samples",
    fill = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_size_dist, "S02_particle_size_distribution.png", width = 10, height = 6)

# Plot C: Boxplot of particle counts by site and PM type
p_count_box <- combined_sample %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = site, y = n_particles, fill = site)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_site, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Particle counts per sample by site and PM type",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of particles per sample (log scale)"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_count_box, "S03_particle_count_boxplot.png", width = 8, height = 5)

# Plot D: Boxplot of particle sizes by site and PM type
p_size_box <- combined_sample %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = site, y = mean_particle_area_um2, fill = site)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_site, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean particle size per sample by site and PM type",
    x = NULL,
    y = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*")")
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_size_box, "S04_particle_size_boxplot.png", width = 8, height = 5)

# ---------------------------
# 2.5 Statistical tests for site differences
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.5 Testing site differences (Mann-Whitney U) ---\n")

site_test_results <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    # Particle count test
    p_value_count = wilcox.test(n_particles ~ site)$p.value,
    # Particle size test
    p_value_size = wilcox.test(mean_particle_area_um2 ~ site)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    count_sig = ifelse(p_value_count < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
    size_sig = ifelse(p_value_size < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

cat("\nSite comparison (NBG vs TCDBG):\n")
print(site_test_results)

write_csv(site_test_results, file.path(tables_dir, "site_comparison_tests.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 2.6 Species richness and sampling effort
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.6 Sampling effort by species ---\n")

sampling_effort <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, species) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    n_years = n_distinct(year),
    years = paste(sort(unique(year)), collapse = ", "),
    total_particles = sum(n_particles),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(site, desc(n_samples))

cat("\nSpecies with most samples (NBG):\n")
sampling_effort %>% filter(site == "NBG") %>% head(10) %>% print()

cat("\nSpecies with most samples (TCDBG):\n")
sampling_effort %>% filter(site == "TCDBG") %>% head(10) %>% print()

write_csv(sampling_effort, file.path(tables_dir, "sampling_effort.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 2.7 Correlation between particle count and size
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 2.7 Correlation between particle count and size ---\n")

cor_test <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    correlation = cor(n_particles, mean_particle_area_um2, method = "spearman"),
    p_value = cor.test(n_particles, mean_particle_area_um2, method = "spearman")$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(significance = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"))

cat("\nSpearman correlation between particle count and size:\n")
print(cor_test)

write_csv(cor_test, file.path(tables_dir, "count_size_correlation.csv"))

# Scatter plot
p_corr <- combined_sample %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_particles, y = mean_particle_area_um2, color = site)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship between particle count and size",
    x = "Particle count per sample (log scale)",
    y = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*", log scale)"),
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_corr, "S05_count_vs_size_correlation.png", width = 10, height = 6)

cat("\n✅ Section 2 complete. Descriptive statistics and overview saved.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 3: SPECIES PERFORMANCE RANKING
# ============================================================
# This section ranks species by:
#   - Mean particle count per sample (n_particles)
#   - Mean particle size per sample (mean_particle_area_um2)
# Includes plots, tables, and statistical tests of species differences.
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                  SECTION 3: SPECIES PERFORMANCE RANKING                       ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 3.1 Species-level summary (mean across samples)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 3.1 Species-level summary ---\n")

species_performance <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type, species) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    mean_particle_count = mean(n_particles, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_particle_count = se(n_particles),
    mean_particle_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_particle_size = se(mean_particle_area_um2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(site, pm_type, desc(mean_particle_count))

cat("\nTop 10 species by mean particle count (TCDBG, PM10):\n")
species_performance %>% filter(site == "TCDBG", pm_type == "PM10") %>% head(10) %>% print()

cat("\nTop 10 species by mean particle count (NBG, PM10):\n")
species_performance %>% filter(site == "NBG", pm_type == "PM10") %>% head(10) %>% print()

write_csv(species_performance, file.path(tables_dir, "species_performance.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 3.2 Statistical test for species differences (Kruskal-Wallis)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 3.2 Testing for significant differences among species ---\n")

species_kw <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_species = n_distinct(species),
    kw_particles_p = kruskal.test(n_particles ~ species)$p.value,
    kw_size_p = kruskal.test(mean_particle_area_um2 ~ species)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    particles_sig = ifelse(kw_particles_p < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
    size_sig = ifelse(kw_size_p < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

cat("\nKruskal-Wallis tests for species effects:\n")
print(species_kw)

write_csv(species_kw, file.path(tables_dir, "species_kruskal_wallis.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 3.3 Ranking plots for particle count (top 10)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 3.3 Generating ranking plots (particle count) ---\n")

# Function to create ranking plot
rank_count_plot <- function(df, site_sel, pm_sel) {
  df_filtered <- df %>%
    filter(site == site_sel, pm_type == pm_sel) %>%
    slice_max(mean_particle_count, n = 10) %>%
    mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species),
           species_pretty = fct_reorder(species_pretty, mean_particle_count))
  
  ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = mean_particle_count, y = species_pretty)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = mean_particle_count), alpha = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = mean_particle_count - se_particle_count,
                      xmax = mean_particle_count + se_particle_count),
                  width = 0.2, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
    labs(
      title = paste(site_sel, "-", pm_sel, ": Top 10 species by particle count"),
      x = "Mean particles per sample (± SE)",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_wtp() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"))
}

# Generate plots for each combination
sites <- c("TCDBG", "NBG")
pm_types <- c("PM10", "PM2.5")

for (s in sites) {
  for (p in pm_types) {
    p_rank_count <- rank_count_plot(species_performance, s, p)
    save_plot_wtp(p_rank_count, paste0("F1_rank_count_", s, "_", p, ".png"), width = 8, height = 6)
    print(p_rank_count)
  }
}

# ---------------------------
# 3.4 Ranking plots for particle size (top 10)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 3.4 Generating ranking plots (particle size) ---\n")

rank_size_plot <- function(df, site_sel, pm_sel) {
  df_filtered <- df %>%
    filter(site == site_sel, pm_type == pm_sel) %>%
    slice_max(mean_particle_size, n = 10) %>%
    mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species),
           species_pretty = fct_reorder(species_pretty, mean_particle_size))
  
  ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = mean_particle_size, y = species_pretty)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = mean_particle_size), alpha = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = mean_particle_size - se_particle_size,
                      xmax = mean_particle_size + se_particle_size),
                  width = 0.2, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma") +
    labs(
      title = paste(site_sel, "-", pm_sel, ": Top 10 species by particle size"),
      x = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*" ± SE)"),
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_wtp() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"))
}

for (s in sites) {
  for (p in pm_types) {
    p_rank_size <- rank_size_plot(species_performance, s, p)
    save_plot_wtp(p_rank_size, paste0("F2_rank_size_", s, "_", p, ".png"), width = 8, height = 6)
    print(p_rank_size)
  }
}

# ---------------------------
# 3.5 Species-level comparison: count vs size (bubble plot)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 3.5 Species comparison: count vs size ---\n")

p_count_vs_size <- species_performance %>%
  filter(n_samples >= 3) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_particle_count, y = mean_particle_size, 
             color = site, size = n_samples)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = pretty_species(species)), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 15) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "n samples") +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  labs(
    title = "Species performance: particle count vs particle size",
    x = "Mean particle count per sample (log scale)",
    y = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*", log scale)"),
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_count_vs_size, "F3_count_vs_size_species.png", width = 12, height = 8)
print(p_count_vs_size)

# ---------------------------
# 3.6 Consistency across years (only species with multiple years)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 3.6 Species consistency across years ---\n")

species_consistency <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, species, pm_type) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(year) >= 2) %>%
  summarise(
    n_years = n_distinct(year),
    cv_particles = sd(n_particles) / mean(n_particles),
    cv_size = sd(mean_particle_area_um2) / mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(cv_particles)

cat("\nMost consistent species (lowest CV in particle count):\n")
species_consistency %>% slice_min(cv_particles, n = 10) %>% print()

write_csv(species_consistency, file.path(tables_dir, "species_consistency.csv"))

cat("\n✅ Section 3 complete. Species ranking saved.\n")
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 4: TRAIT ANALYSES (H1, H2, H4)
# ============================================================
# Tests whether traits affect:
#   - Particle count (n_particles) – "how much PM"
#   - Particle size (mean_particle_area_um2) – "how large PM"
# Traits: evergreen, trichomes, surface_texture, leaf_size_class
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                         SECTION 4: TRAIT ANALYSES                              ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 4.1 Helper functions for trait tests
# ---------------------------
test_binary_trait <- function(df, trait_col, outcome_col, outcome_name) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_col]])) %>%
    group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
    summarise(
      n1 = sum(.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[1]),
      n2 = sum(.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[2]),
      mean1 = mean(.data[[outcome_col]][.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[1]], na.rm = TRUE),
      mean2 = mean(.data[[outcome_col]][.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[2]], na.rm = TRUE),
      p_value = wilcox.test(.data[[outcome_col]] ~ .data[[trait_col]])$p.value,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      trait = trait_col,
      outcome = outcome_name,
      significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
    )
}

test_multiclass_trait <- function(df, trait_col, outcome_col, outcome_name) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_col]])) %>%
    group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
    summarise(
      p_value = kruskal.test(.data[[outcome_col]] ~ .data[[trait_col]])$p.value,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      trait = trait_col,
      outcome = outcome_name,
      significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
    )
}

# ---------------------------
# 4.2 Evergreen vs Deciduous (H2)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 4.2 Evergreen vs Deciduous ---\n")

# For particle count
evergreen_count <- test_binary_trait(combined_sample, "evergreen", "n_particles", "Particle count")
cat("\nEvergreen effect on particle count:\n")
print(evergreen_count)

# For particle size
evergreen_size <- test_binary_trait(combined_sample, "evergreen", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")
cat("\nEvergreen effect on particle size:\n")
print(evergreen_size)

# Combine
evergreen_results <- bind_rows(evergreen_count, evergreen_size)
write_csv(evergreen_results, file.path(tables_dir, "trait_evergreen.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 4.3 Trichomes (H1)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 4.3 Trichomes effect ---\n")

trichomes_count <- test_binary_trait(combined_sample, "trichomes", "n_particles", "Particle count")
trichomes_size <- test_binary_trait(combined_sample, "trichomes", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")
trichomes_results <- bind_rows(trichomes_count, trichomes_size)
write_csv(trichomes_results, file.path(tables_dir, "trait_trichomes.csv"))

cat("\nTrichomes effect on particle count:\n")
print(trichomes_count)

# ---------------------------
# 4.4 Surface texture (H1)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 4.4 Surface texture effect ---\n")

texture_count <- test_multiclass_trait(combined_sample, "surface_texture", "n_particles", "Particle count")
texture_size <- test_multiclass_trait(combined_sample, "surface_texture", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")
texture_results <- bind_rows(texture_count, texture_size)
write_csv(texture_results, file.path(tables_dir, "trait_surface_texture.csv"))

cat("\nSurface texture effect (Kruskal-Wallis p-values):\n")
print(texture_results)

# ---------------------------
# 4.5 Leaf size class (H4 - expected no strong effect)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 4.5 Leaf size class effect ---\n")

leafsize_count <- test_multiclass_trait(combined_sample, "leaf_size_class", "n_particles", "Particle count")
leafsize_size <- test_multiclass_trait(combined_sample, "leaf_size_class", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")
leafsize_results <- bind_rows(leafsize_count, leafsize_size)
write_csv(leafsize_results, file.path(tables_dir, "trait_leaf_size.csv"))

cat("\nLeaf size class effect (Kruskal-Wallis p-values):\n")
print(leafsize_results)

# Leaf size class effect on particle count - boxplot
p_leafsize_count <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(leaf_size_class)) %>%
  mutate(leaf_size_class = factor(leaf_size_class, 
                                   levels = c("nano", "micro", "noto", "meso"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = leaf_size_class, y = n_particles, fill = leaf_size_class)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  facet_grid(site ~ pm_type) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", guide = "none") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Leaf size class effect on particle count",
    x = "Leaf size class (Ellis et al., 2009)",
    y = "Particles per sample (log scale)"
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

save_plot_wtp(p_leafsize_count, "F_leafsize_count.png", width = 10, height = 8)

# ---------------------------
# 4.6 Visualisation: trait effects (boxplots)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 4.6 Generating trait effect boxplots ---\n")

# Boxplot function for binary traits
plot_binary_trait <- function(df, trait_col, outcome_col, outcome_label, palette) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_col]])) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[trait_col]], y = .data[[outcome_col]], fill = .data[[trait_col]])) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
    facet_grid(site ~ pm_type) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette, guide = "none") +
    scale_y_log10() +
    labs(
      title = paste(str_to_title(trait_col), "effect on", outcome_label),
      x = NULL,
      y = ifelse(outcome_label == "Particle count", "Particles per sample (log scale)", 
                 expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*", log scale)"))
    ) +
    theme_wtp()
}

# Evergreen plots
p_evergreen_count <- plot_binary_trait(combined_sample, "evergreen", "n_particles", "Particle count", pal_evergreen)
p_evergreen_size <- plot_binary_trait(combined_sample, "evergreen", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size", pal_evergreen)
save_plot_wtp(p_evergreen_count, "F4_evergreen_count.png", width = 8, height = 6)
save_plot_wtp(p_evergreen_size, "F5_evergreen_size.png", width = 8, height = 6)

# Trichomes plots
p_trichomes_count <- plot_binary_trait(combined_sample, "trichomes", "n_particles", "Particle count", pal_trichomes)
p_trichomes_size <- plot_binary_trait(combined_sample, "trichomes", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size", pal_trichomes)
save_plot_wtp(p_trichomes_count, "F6_trichomes_count.png", width = 8, height = 6)
save_plot_wtp(p_trichomes_size, "F7_trichomes_size.png", width = 8, height = 6)

# Surface texture boxplot (multi-class)
plot_multi_trait <- function(df, trait_col, outcome_col, outcome_label) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_col]])) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[trait_col]], y = .data[[outcome_col]], fill = .data[[trait_col]])) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
    facet_grid(site ~ pm_type) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", guide = "none") +
    scale_y_log10() +
    labs(
      title = paste(str_to_title(gsub("_", " ", trait_col)), "effect on", outcome_label),
      x = NULL,
      y = ifelse(outcome_label == "Particle count", "Particles per sample (log scale)",
                 expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*", log scale)"))
    ) +
    theme_wtp() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}

p_texture_count <- plot_multi_trait(combined_sample, "surface_texture", "n_particles", "Particle count")
p_texture_size <- plot_multi_trait(combined_sample, "surface_texture", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")
save_plot_wtp(p_texture_count, "F8_texture_count.png", width = 8, height = 6)
save_plot_wtp(p_texture_size, "F9_texture_size.png", width = 8, height = 6)

cat("\n✅ Section 4 complete. Trait analyses saved.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 5: LEAF ANGLE vs PARTICLE SIZE (H3)
# ============================================================
# Primary hypothesis: steeper leaf angles → larger particles
# Analysis: correlation, regression, quartile comparison
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n              SECTION 5: LEAF ANGLE vs PARTICLE SIZE (H3)                      ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 5.1 Sample-level correlation
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 5.1 Sample-level correlation (Spearman) ---\n")

angle_corr_sample <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_angle), !is.na(mean_particle_area_um2)) %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    spearman_r = cor(mean_angle, mean_particle_area_um2, method = "spearman"),
    p_value = cor.test(mean_angle, mean_particle_area_um2, method = "spearman")$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    direction = ifelse(spearman_r > 0, "Positive (steeper → larger)", "Negative (steeper → smaller)")
  )

cat("\nLeaf angle vs particle size correlation (sample-level):\n")
print(angle_corr_sample)

write_csv(angle_corr_sample, file.path(tables_dir, "leaf_angle_correlation_sample.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 5.2 Species-level correlation (aggregated to avoid pseudoreplication)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 5.2 Species-level correlation ---\n")

species_angle <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_angle), !is.na(mean_particle_area_um2)) %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type, species, mean_angle) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_particle_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    n_samples = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_samples >= 2)

angle_corr_species <- species_angle %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_species = n(),
    spearman_r = cor(mean_angle, mean_particle_size, method = "spearman"),
    p_value = cor.test(mean_angle, mean_particle_size, method = "spearman")$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))

cat("\nLeaf angle vs particle size correlation (species-level):\n")
print(angle_corr_species)

write_csv(angle_corr_species, file.path(tables_dir, "leaf_angle_correlation_species.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 5.3 Linear regression (species-level)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 5.3 Linear regression (species-level) ---\n")

regression_results <- species_angle %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    slope = coef(lm(mean_particle_size ~ mean_angle))[2],
    r_squared = summary(lm(mean_particle_size ~ mean_angle))$r.squared,
    p_value = summary(lm(mean_particle_size ~ mean_angle))$coefficients[2, 4],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nRegression results (particle size ~ leaf angle):\n")
print(regression_results)

write_csv(regression_results, file.path(tables_dir, "leaf_angle_regression.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 5.4 Leaf angle quartile analysis
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 5.4 Leaf angle quartile analysis ---\n")

angle_quartile <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_angle), !is.na(mean_particle_area_um2)) %>%
  mutate(
    angle_quartile = ntile(mean_angle, 4),
    angle_group = case_when(
      angle_quartile == 1 ~ "Q1 (shallowest)",
      angle_quartile == 2 ~ "Q2",
      angle_quartile == 3 ~ "Q3",
      angle_quartile == 4 ~ "Q4 (steepest)"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type, angle_group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_particle_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    se_particle_size = se(mean_particle_area_um2),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nParticle size by leaf angle quartile:\n")
print(angle_quartile)

write_csv(angle_quartile, file.path(tables_dir, "leaf_angle_quartile.csv"))

# Combined leaf angle correlations - both count and size
angle_corr_count <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_angle), !is.na(n_particles)) %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    spearman_r = round(cor(mean_angle, n_particles, method = "spearman"), 3),
    p_value = round(cor.test(mean_angle, n_particles, method = "spearman")$p.value, 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(outcome = "Particle count",
         significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))

angle_corr_size <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_angle), !is.na(mean_particle_area_um2)) %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    spearman_r = round(cor(mean_angle, mean_particle_area_um2, method = "spearman"), 3),
    p_value = round(cor.test(mean_angle, mean_particle_area_um2, method = "spearman")$p.value, 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(outcome = "Particle size",
         significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))

angle_corr_combined <- bind_rows(angle_corr_count, angle_corr_size) %>%
  arrange(outcome, site, pm_type)

print(angle_corr_combined, width = Inf)
write_csv(angle_corr_combined, file.path(tables_dir, "leaf_angle_correlations_combined.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 5.5 Scatter plots with regression lines
# ---------------------------
p_angle_both <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_angle)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(n_particles, mean_particle_area_um2),
               names_to = "outcome",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(outcome_label = ifelse(outcome == "n_particles",
                                "Particle count", 
                                "Mean particle size (µm²)")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_angle, y = value, color = site)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  facet_grid(outcome_label ~ pm_type, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Leaf angle vs particle count and particle size",
    x = "Mean leaf angle (degrees)",
    y = "Outcome (log scale)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_angle_both, "F10_leaf_angle_both_outcomes.png", width = 12, height = 8)
print(p_angle_scatter)

# Quartile bar plot
p_angle_quartile <- angle_quartile %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = angle_group, y = mean_particle_size, fill = site)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_particle_size - se_particle_size,
                    ymax = mean_particle_size + se_particle_size),
                position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "Particle size by leaf angle quartile",
    x = "Leaf angle quartile",
    y = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*")"),
    fill = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_angle_quartile, "F11_leaf_angle_quartile.png", width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_angle_quartile)






cat("\n✅ Section 5 complete. Leaf angle analyses saved.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 6: YEAR-TO-YEAR VARIABILITY AND STABILITY
# ============================================================
# Assesses which species are consistent across years in:
#   - Particle count
#   - Particle size
# Uses coefficient of variation (CV)
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n              SECTION 6: YEAR-TO-YEAR VARIABILITY AND STABILITY                 ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 6.1 Species stability (CV) for particle count
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 6.1 Species stability for particle count ---\n")

stability_count <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, species, pm_type) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(year) >= 2) %>%
  summarise(
    n_years = n_distinct(year),
    mean_count = mean(n_particles),
    sd_count = sd(n_particles),
    cv_count = sd_count / mean_count,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(cv_count)

cat("\nMost stable species (lowest CV) for particle count:\n")
stability_count %>% slice_min(cv_count, n = 10) %>% print()

cat("\nLeast stable species (highest CV) for particle count:\n")
stability_count %>% slice_max(cv_count, n = 10) %>% print()

write_csv(stability_count, file.path(tables_dir, "stability_particle_count.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 6.2 Species stability (CV) for particle size
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 6.2 Species stability for particle size ---\n")

stability_size <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, species, pm_type) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(year) >= 2) %>%
  summarise(
    n_years = n_distinct(year),
    mean_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    sd_size = sd(mean_particle_area_um2),
    cv_size = sd_size / mean_size,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(cv_size)

cat("\nMost stable species (lowest CV) for particle size:\n")
stability_size %>% slice_min(cv_size, n = 10) %>% print()

cat("\nLeast stable species (highest CV) for particle size:\n")
stability_size %>% slice_max(cv_size, n = 10) %>% print()

write_csv(stability_size, file.path(tables_dir, "stability_particle_size.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 6.3 Year trends: mean particle count over time
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 6.3 Yearly trends in particle count ---\n")

year_trend_count <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, year, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count = mean(n_particles),
    se_count = se(n_particles),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_trend_count <- year_trend_count %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = mean_count, color = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_count - se_count, ymax = mean_count + se_count), width = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title = "Yearly trends in mean particle count per sample",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean particle count (log scale)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_trend_count, "F12_year_trend_count.png", width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_trend_count)

# ---------------------------
# 6.4 Year trends: mean particle size over time
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 6.4 Yearly trends in particle size ---\n")

year_trend_size <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, year, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    se_size = se(mean_particle_area_um2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_trend_size <- year_trend_size %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = mean_size, color = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_size - se_size, ymax = mean_size + se_size), width = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "Yearly trends in mean particle size per sample",
    x = "Year",
    y = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*")"),
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_trend_size, "F13_year_trend_size.png", width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_trend_size)

# ---------------------------
# 6.5 Species-specific trajectories (example: top 6 species by count)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 6.5 Species-specific trajectories (top species) ---\n")

# Select top species by overall mean count for each site
top_species <- species_performance %>%
  filter(pm_type == "PM10") %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  slice_max(mean_particle_count, n = 6) %>%
  pull(species) %>% unique()

traj_data <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(species %in% top_species, pm_type == "PM10") %>%
  group_by(site, species, year) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count = mean(n_particles),
    se_count = se(n_particles),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_trajectories <- traj_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = mean_count, color = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_count - se_count, ymax = mean_count + se_count), width = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Year-to-year trajectories for top species (PM10)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean particle count per sample (log scale)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "italic"))

save_plot_wtp(p_trajectories, "F14_species_trajectories.png", width = 12, height = 8)
print(p_trajectories)

cat("\n✅ Section 6 complete. Stability and trends saved.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 7: PM2.5:PM10 RATIOS (H5) - FIXED
# ============================================================
# Compares PM2.5 to PM10 for:
#   - Particle count ratio
#   - Particle size ratio
# Identifies species with high PM2.5 selectivity
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                  SECTION 7: PM2.5:PM10 RATIOS (H5)                            ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 7.1 Aggregate per species-year-site-pm_type first
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 7.1 Aggregating per species-year-site-PM type ---\n")

# Average particle count and size for each species-year-site-PM type
agg_pm <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, year, species, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count = mean(n_particles, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Now pivot to get PM10 and PM2.5 columns
ratio_data <- agg_pm %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(site, year, species),
    names_from = pm_type,
    values_from = c(mean_count, mean_size),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mean_count_PM10), !is.na(mean_count_PM2.5),
         !is.na(mean_size_PM10), !is.na(mean_size_PM2.5)) %>%
  mutate(
    count_ratio = mean_count_PM2.5 / mean_count_PM10,
    size_ratio = mean_size_PM2.5 / mean_size_PM10
  )

cat("Number of species-year-site combinations with both PM fractions:", nrow(ratio_data), "\n")

# ---------------------------
# 7.2 Summary of ratios by site and year
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 7.2 Summary of ratios ---\n")

ratio_summary <- ratio_data %>%
  group_by(site, year) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count_ratio = mean(count_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_count_ratio = se(count_ratio),
    mean_size_ratio = mean(size_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_size_ratio = se(size_ratio),
    n_species = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nRatio summary by site and year:\n")
print(ratio_summary)

write_csv(ratio_summary, file.path(tables_dir, "pm_ratios_summary.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 7.3 Species with highest PM2.5 selectivity (count ratio > 0.7)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 7.3 Species with high PM2.5 selectivity (count ratio > 0.7) ---\n")

high_selectivity <- ratio_data %>%
  filter(count_ratio > 0.7) %>%
  arrange(desc(count_ratio)) %>%
  select(site, year, species, count_ratio, size_ratio)

cat("\nSpecies with count ratio > 0.7 (more PM2.5 than PM10 by count):\n")
print(high_selectivity)

write_csv(high_selectivity, file.path(tables_dir, "high_pm25_selectivity.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 7.4 Visualisation: count ratio by species
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 7.4 Generating ratio plots ---\n")

p_count_ratio <- ratio_data %>%
  mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(species_pretty, count_ratio), y = count_ratio, color = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "darkred", alpha = 0.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ year, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "PM2.5:PM10 particle count ratio by species and year",
    x = NULL,
    y = "PM2.5 count / PM10 count",
    color = "Site",
    caption = "Red dashed line = 0.5 (balanced), dotted line = 1 (equal)"
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 8))

save_plot_wtp(p_count_ratio, "F15_pm_count_ratio.png", width = 12, height = 10)
print(p_count_ratio)

# Size ratio plot
p_size_ratio <- ratio_data %>%
  mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(species_pretty, size_ratio), y = size_ratio, color = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ year, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "PM2.5:PM10 particle size ratio by species and year",
    x = NULL,
    y = "PM2.5 mean size / PM10 mean size",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 8))

save_plot_wtp(p_size_ratio, "F16_pm_size_ratio.png", width = 12, height = 10)
print(p_size_ratio)

# ---------------------------
# 7.5 Statistical test for site differences in ratios (fixed)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 7.5 Site differences in ratios ---\n")

# Get years where both sites have data
years_with_both <- ratio_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(n_sites = n_distinct(site), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_sites == 2) %>%
  pull(year)

if (length(years_with_both) > 0) {
  ratio_site_test <- ratio_data %>%
    filter(year %in% years_with_both) %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    summarise(
      count_p = wilcox.test(count_ratio ~ site)$p.value,
      size_p = wilcox.test(size_ratio ~ site)$p.value,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      count_sig = ifelse(count_p < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
      size_sig = ifelse(size_p < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
    )
  
  cat("\nSite comparison of ratios (years with both sites):\n")
  print(ratio_site_test)
  write_csv(ratio_site_test, file.path(tables_dir, "ratio_site_tests.csv"))
} else {
  cat("\nNo years with data from both sites for ratio comparison.\n")
}
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 8: MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS (MIXED MODELS)
# ============================================================
# Tests H1-H6 using linear mixed models:
#   - Outcome 1: log(n_particles) (particle count)
#   - Outcome 2: log(mean_particle_area_um2) (particle size)
# Fixed effects: pm_type, site, evergreen, trichomes, surface_texture, mean_angle
# Random intercept: species
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                 SECTION 8: MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS (MIXED MODELS)                ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 8.1 Prepare model data
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 8.1 Preparing model data ---\n")

model_data <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(evergreen), !is.na(trichomes), !is.na(surface_texture),
    !is.na(mean_angle), !is.na(leaf_size_class)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    log_count = log(n_particles + 1),
    log_size = log(mean_particle_area_um2 + 1),
    evergreen_bin = ifelse(evergreen == "Evergreen", 1, 0),
    trichomes_bin = ifelse(trichomes == "Yes", 1, 0),
    mean_angle_scaled = scale(mean_angle),
    pm_type = factor(pm_type),
    site = factor(site),
    surface_texture = factor(surface_texture),
    leaf_size_class = factor(leaf_size_class)
  )

cat("Complete cases for models:", nrow(model_data), "\n")
cat("Number of species:", n_distinct(model_data$species), "\n")

# ---------------------------
# 8.2 Model A: Particle count (log_count)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 8.2 Model A: Particle count (log_count) ---\n")

# Full model
model_count_full <- lmer(log_count ~ pm_type + site + evergreen + trichomes + 
                           surface_texture + mean_angle_scaled + leaf_size_class +
                           (1 | species), 
                         data = model_data, REML = FALSE,
                         control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5)))

# Null model (species only)
model_count_null <- lmer(log_count ~ 1 + (1 | species), data = model_data, REML = FALSE)

# Summary
cat("\nModel A summary (particle count):\n")
print(summary(model_count_full))

# Likelihood ratio test for species random effect
lrt_count <- anova(model_count_null, model_count_full, test = "Chisq")
cat("\nLikelihood ratio test (species random effect):\n")
print(lrt_count)

# Extract fixed effects
count_fixed <- broom.mixed::tidy(model_count_full, effects = "fixed") %>%
  mutate(significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))

write_csv(count_fixed, file.path(tables_dir, "model_count_coefficients.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 8.3 Model B: Particle size (log_size)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 8.3 Model B: Particle size (log_size) ---\n")

model_size_full <- lmer(log_size ~ pm_type + site + evergreen + trichomes + 
                          surface_texture + mean_angle_scaled + leaf_size_class +
                          (1 | species), 
                        data = model_data, REML = FALSE,
                        control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e5)))

model_size_null <- lmer(log_size ~ 1 + (1 | species), data = model_data, REML = FALSE)

cat("\nModel B summary (particle size):\n")
print(summary(model_size_full))

lrt_size <- anova(model_size_null, model_size_full, test = "Chisq")
cat("\nLikelihood ratio test (species random effect):\n")
print(lrt_size)

size_fixed <- broom.mixed::tidy(model_size_full, effects = "fixed") %>%
  mutate(significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))

write_csv(size_fixed, file.path(tables_dir, "model_size_coefficients.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 8.4 Variance partitioning (H6)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 8.4 Variance partitioning (species identity) ---\n")

# Extract variance components
var_count <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model_count_full))
var_size <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model_size_full))

total_var_count <- sum(var_count$vcov)
species_var_count <- var_count$vcov[1]
residual_var_count <- var_count$vcov[2]

total_var_size <- sum(var_size$vcov)
species_var_size <- var_size$vcov[1]
residual_var_size <- var_size$vcov[2]

cat("\nParticle count model:\n")
cat("  Species explains", round(species_var_count / total_var_count * 100, 1), "% of variance\n")
cat("  Residual variance:", round(residual_var_count / total_var_count * 100, 1), "%\n")

cat("\nParticle size model:\n")
cat("  Species explains", round(species_var_size / total_var_size * 100, 1), "% of variance\n")
cat("  Residual variance:", round(residual_var_size / total_var_size * 100, 1), "%\n")

variance_partitioning <- data.frame(
  Outcome = c("Particle count", "Particle size"),
  Species_variance_pct = c(round(species_var_count / total_var_count * 100, 1),
                           round(species_var_size / total_var_size * 100, 1)),
  Residual_variance_pct = c(round(residual_var_count / total_var_count * 100, 1),
                            round(residual_var_size / total_var_size * 100, 1))
)
write_csv(variance_partitioning, file.path(tables_dir, "variance_partitioning.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 8.5 Model diagnostics
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 8.5 Model diagnostics ---\n")

# Check residuals
residuals_count <- residuals(model_count_full)
residuals_size <- residuals(model_size_full)

# QQ plots
png(file.path(figures_dir, "model_diagnostics.png"), width = 10, height = 5, units = "in", res = 300)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
qqnorm(residuals_count, main = "Particle count model residuals")
qqline(residuals_count, col = "red")
qqnorm(residuals_size, main = "Particle size model residuals")
qqline(residuals_size, col = "red")
dev.off()
cat("  Saved model diagnostics plot\n")

# ---------------------------
# 8.6 Coefficient forest plots
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 8.6 Coefficient forest plots ---\n")

# Forest plot function
forest_plot <- function(tidy_df, title) {
  tidy_df %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(term = str_replace_all(term, "_", " ") %>% str_to_sentence()) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate), 
               xmin = estimate - std.error, xmax = estimate + std.error)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(size = 3, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_errorbar(width = 0.2, color = "steelblue") +
    labs(title = title, x = "Estimate (log scale)", y = NULL) +
    theme_wtp()
}

p_forest_count <- forest_plot(count_fixed, "Fixed effects: Particle count model")
p_forest_size <- forest_plot(size_fixed, "Fixed effects: Particle size model")

save_plot_wtp(p_forest_count, "F17_forest_count.png", width = 8, height = 6)
save_plot_wtp(p_forest_size, "F18_forest_size.png", width = 8, height = 6)

cat("\n✅ Section 8 complete. Mixed models and variance partitioning saved.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 9: CORRELATION MATRIX (FINAL FIX)
# ============================================================
cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                      SECTION 9: CORRELATION MATRIX                            ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# Select all needed columns, including the ones for dummy variables
cor_vars <- combined_sample %>%
  select(n_particles, mean_particle_area_um2, mean_angle, year,
         pm_type, site, evergreen, trichomes) %>%
  mutate(
    pm10_dummy = ifelse(pm_type == "PM10", 1, 0),
    site_dummy = ifelse(site == "TCDBG", 1, 0),
    evergreen_dummy = ifelse(evergreen == "Evergreen", 1, 0),
    trichomes_dummy = ifelse(trichomes == "Yes", 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  # Keep only numeric columns for correlation
  select(n_particles, mean_particle_area_um2, mean_angle, year,
         pm10_dummy, site_dummy, evergreen_dummy, trichomes_dummy)

# Compute correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(cor_vars, use = "complete.obs")
cor_pvalues <- rcorr(as.matrix(cor_vars))$P

# Save and print
write_csv(as.data.frame(cor_matrix), file.path(tables_dir, "correlation_matrix.csv"))
cat("\nCorrelation matrix (numeric variables):\n")
print(round(cor_matrix, 3))

# Heatmap
png(file.path(figures_dir, "F19_correlation_heatmap.png"), width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper", 
         tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45, 
         addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.7,
         title = "Correlation matrix", mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0))
dev.off()
cat("  Saved correlation heatmap\n")

# Focused correlations
focused_cor <- cor_matrix[c("n_particles", "mean_particle_area_um2"), 
                          c("mean_angle", "year", "pm10_dummy", "site_dummy", 
                            "evergreen_dummy", "trichomes_dummy")]
cat("\nFocused correlations with outcomes:\n")
print(round(focused_cor, 3))
write_csv(as.data.frame(focused_cor), file.path(tables_dir, "focused_correlations.csv"))

cat("\n✅ Section 9 complete. Correlation matrix saved.\n")

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 10: SITE COMPARISON (TCDBG vs NBG)
# ============================================================
# Direct comparison of particle count and size between sites
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                     SECTION 10: SITE COMPARISON                               ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 10.1 Overall site comparison (all years combined)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 10.1 Overall site comparison ---\n")

site_overall <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count = mean(n_particles),
    se_count = se(n_particles),
    mean_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    se_size = se(mean_particle_area_um2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nOverall means by site and PM type:\n")
print(site_overall)

# Statistical tests
site_tests <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(pm_type) %>%
  summarise(
    count_p = wilcox.test(n_particles ~ site)$p.value,
    size_p = wilcox.test(mean_particle_area_um2 ~ site)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    count_sig = ifelse(count_p < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
    size_sig = ifelse(size_p < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

cat("\nWilcoxon tests for site differences:\n")
print(site_tests)

write_csv(site_tests, file.path(tables_dir, "site_comparison_tests_overall.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 10.2 Site comparison by year
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 10.2 Site comparison by year ---\n")

site_year <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(year, pm_type, site) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count = mean(n_particles),
    se_count = se(n_particles),
    mean_size = mean(mean_particle_area_um2),
    se_size = se(mean_particle_area_um2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot: particle count by site and year
p_site_count <- site_year %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = mean_count, color = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_count - se_count, ymax = mean_count + se_count), width = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Site comparison: particle count over years",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean particle count per sample (log scale)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_site_count, "F20_site_count_comparison.png", width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_site_count)

# Plot: particle size by site and year
p_site_size <- site_year %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = mean_size, color = site, group = site)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_size - se_size, 
                    ymax = mean_size + se_size), width = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ pm_type, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "Site comparison: mean particle size over years",
    x = "Year",
    y = expression("Mean particle area ("*mu*"m"^2*")"),
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_site_size, "F21_site_size_comparison.png", width = 10, height = 6)

cat("\n✅ Section 10 complete. Site comparisons saved.\n")
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 11: COMPOSITE SCORES AND RECOMMENDATIONS
# ============================================================
# Creates a composite score for each species combining:
#   - Particle count (higher is better)
#   - Consistency (low CV, higher is better)
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n              SECTION 11: COMPOSITE SCORES AND RECOMMENDATIONS                 ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 11.1 Calculate species-level scores
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 11.1 Calculating composite scores ---\n")

# Get species-level means and CVs
# ---------------------------
# 11.1 Calculate species-level scores
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 11.1 Calculating composite scores ---\n")

# CV based on combined PM10 + PM2.5 count averaged per year
stability_both <- combined_sample %>%
  group_by(site, species, year) %>%
  summarise(mean_count_year = mean(n_particles), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(site, species) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(year) >= 2) %>%
  summarise(
    n_years = n_distinct(year),
    cv_both = sd(mean_count_year) / mean(mean_count_year),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Rebuild composite with corrected consistency
species_scores_bothPM <- species_performance %>%
  group_by(site, species) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_count_both = mean(mean_particle_count),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(stability_both %>% select(site, species, cv_both),
            by = c("site", "species")) %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  mutate(
    score_count = percent_rank(mean_count_both) * 100,
    score_consistency = percent_rank(-replace_na(cv_both, 1)) * 100,
    composite = (score_count + score_consistency) / 2
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(site, desc(composite))

# Keep species_scores pointing to the new object so downstream code still works
species_scores <- species_scores_bothPM

cat("\nTop 10 species by composite score (TCDBG):\n")
species_scores %>%
  filter(site == "TCDBG") %>%
  head(10) %>%
  select(species, composite, score_count, score_consistency,
         mean_count_both) %>%
  print(width = Inf)

cat("\nTop 10 species by composite score (NBG):\n")
species_scores %>%
  filter(site == "NBG") %>%
  head(10) %>%
  select(species, composite, score_count, score_consistency,
         mean_count_both) %>%
  print(width = Inf)

write_csv(species_scores, file.path(tables_dir, "species_composite_scores.csv"))


# ---------------------------
# 11.2 Plot composite scores
# ---------------------------
p_composite <- species_scores %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  slice_max(composite, n = 10) %>%
  mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species),
         species_pretty = fct_reorder(species_pretty, composite)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = composite, y = species_pretty, fill = site)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ site, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_site, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 species by composite PM capture score",
    subtitle = "Score integrates mean particle count (PM10 + PM2.5) and temporal consistency (equal weights)",
    x = "Composite score (0–100)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"))

save_plot_wtp(p_composite, "F22_composite_scores.png", width = 10, height = 8)
print(p_composite)
cat("\n✅ Section 11 complete. Composite scores saved.\n")
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 12: FINAL SUMMARY AND KEY FINDINGS
# ============================================================
# Comprehensive summary of all results, hypothesis testing outcomes,
# and recommendations.
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n                    SECTION 12: FINAL SUMMARY AND KEY FINDINGS                 ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 12.1 Hypothesis testing summary
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 12.1 Hypothesis testing summary ---\n")

hypothesis_summary <- data.frame(
  Hypothesis = c("H1", "H2", "H3", "H4", "H5", "H6"),
  Description = c(
    "Trichomes and surface texture increase PM capture",
    "Evergreen species capture more PM than deciduous",
    "Steeper leaf angles intercept larger particles",
    "Leaf size shows no clear relationship with PM capture",
    "Species differ in PM2.5 selectivity",
    "Species identity explains significant variance"
  ),
  Supported = c(
    ifelse(any(trichomes_results$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(any(evergreen_results$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(any(angle_corr_sample$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(all(leafsize_results$p_value > 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "Yes", "No"),
    "Yes (see ratios)",
    ifelse(lrt_count$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2] < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ),
  Key_Finding = c(
    "Trichomes significantly increased particle count in some site/PM combinations",
    "Evergreen effect varied by site; significant for NBG PM10",
    "Positive correlation at NBG site (steeper angle → larger particles)",
    "No significant leaf size effect detected",
    "Substantial variation among species in PM2.5:PM10 ratios",
    paste("Species explained", round(species_var_count / total_var_count * 100, 1), "% of count variance")
  )
)

print(hypothesis_summary)
write_csv(hypothesis_summary, file.path(tables_dir, "hypothesis_summary.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 12.2 Top performing species recommendations
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 12.2 Top performing species recommendations ---\n")

top_recommendations <- species_scores %>%
  filter(pm_type == "PM10") %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  slice_max(composite, n = 5) %>%
  select(site, species, composite, mean_particle_count, mean_particle_size, score_consistency)

cat("\nTop 5 recommended species for PM mitigation (based on composite score):\n")
print(top_recommendations)

write_csv(top_recommendations, file.path(tables_dir, "top_recommendations.csv"))

# ---------------------------
# 12.3 Summary of outputs
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 12.3 Output files summary ---\n")

cat("\nAll outputs saved to:\n")
cat("  Tables:", tables_dir, "\n")
cat("  Figures:", figures_dir, "\n")

cat("\nKey tables generated:\n")
table_files <- list.files(tables_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$")
for (tf in table_files) {
  cat("  -", tf, "\n")
}

cat("\nKey figures generated:\n")
figure_files <- list.files(figures_dir, pattern = "\\.png$")
for (ff in figure_files) {
  cat("  -", ff, "\n")
}

cat("\n✅ Section 12 complete. Final summary saved.\n")
cat("\n==========================================\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE. All outputs ready.\n")
cat("==========================================\n")
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 13: ADDITIONAL HIGH-QUALITY ANALYSES AND FIGURES
# ============================================================
# This section adds:
# 1. Effect sizes for binary traits (Cohen's d) with forest plots
# 2. Pairwise comparisons for multi-level traits (output as tables)
# 3. Mixed model diagnostic plots (residuals vs fitted)
# 4. PCA biplot of species performance
# 5. Heatmap of species × year particle counts
# 6. Map of tree locations with composite scores (using QGIS data)
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n        SECTION 13: ADDITIONAL HIGH-QUALITY ANALYSES AND FIGURES               ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# ---------------------------
# 13.1 Helper function for Cohen's d
# ---------------------------
cohens_d <- function(x, y) {
  nx <- length(x)
  ny <- length(y)
  pooled_sd <- sqrt(((nx - 1) * var(x) + (ny - 1) * var(y)) / (nx + ny - 2))
  (mean(x) - mean(y)) / pooled_sd
}

# ---------------------------
# 13.2 Effect sizes for binary traits (Evergreen, Trichomes)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 13.1 Computing effect sizes (Cohen's d) for binary traits ---\n")

effect_size_binary <- function(df, trait_col, outcome_col) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_col]]), !is.na(.data[[outcome_col]])) %>%
    group_by(site, pm_type) %>%
    summarise(
      n1 = sum(.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[1]),
      n2 = sum(.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[2]),
      mean1 = mean(.data[[outcome_col]][.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[1]], na.rm = TRUE),
      mean2 = mean(.data[[outcome_col]][.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[2]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd1 = sd(.data[[outcome_col]][.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[1]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd2 = sd(.data[[outcome_col]][.data[[trait_col]] == levels(factor(.data[[trait_col]]))[2]], na.rm = TRUE),
      cohens_d = (mean1 - mean2) / sqrt(((n1-1)*sd1^2 + (n2-1)*sd2^2) / (n1+n2-2)),
      p_value = wilcox.test(.data[[outcome_col]] ~ .data[[trait_col]])$p.value,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      ci_lower = cohens_d - 1.96 * sqrt((n1+n2)/(n1*n2) + cohens_d^2/(2*(n1+n2))),
      ci_upper = cohens_d + 1.96 * sqrt((n1+n2)/(n1*n2) + cohens_d^2/(2*(n1+n2)))
    )
}

# Evergreen effect sizes for particle count
evergreen_es_count <- effect_size_binary(combined_sample, "evergreen", "n_particles") %>%
  mutate(trait = "Evergreen", outcome = "Particle count")

# Evergreen effect sizes for particle size
evergreen_es_size <- effect_size_binary(combined_sample, "evergreen", "mean_particle_area_um2") %>%
  mutate(trait = "Evergreen", outcome = "Particle size")

# Trichomes effect sizes
trichomes_es_count <- effect_size_binary(combined_sample, "trichomes", "n_particles") %>%
  mutate(trait = "Trichomes", outcome = "Particle count")
trichomes_es_size <- effect_size_binary(combined_sample, "trichomes", "mean_particle_area_um2") %>%
  mutate(trait = "Trichomes", outcome = "Particle size")

# Combine all effect sizes
```
