```{r}
all_effect_sizes <- bind_rows(evergreen_es_count, evergreen_es_size,
                              trichomes_es_count, trichomes_es_size) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_label = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "p < 0.05", "p ≥ 0.05"),
    group_label = paste(site, pm_type, outcome, sep = " | ")
  )

# Forest plot of effect sizes
p_effect_forest <- all_effect_sizes %>%
  filter(!is.na(cohens_d)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cohens_d, y = reorder(group_label, cohens_d), 
             xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper, color = sig_label)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(width = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ trait, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("p < 0.05" = "red", "p ≥ 0.05" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    title = "Effect sizes (Cohen's d) for binary traits",
    subtitle = "Positive d indicates higher values for the second level (Evergreen/Yes)",
    x = "Cohen's d (95% CI)",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

save_plot_wtp(p_effect_forest, "F23_effect_sizes_forest.png", width = 10, height = 8)

# ---------------------------
# 13.3 Pairwise comparisons for multi-level traits (tables only)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 13.2 Pairwise tests for surface texture and leaf size class (tables) ---\n")

pairwise_table <- function(df, trait_col, outcome_col, outcome_name) {
  dat <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[trait_col]]), !is.na(.data[[outcome_col]])) %>%
    mutate(group = as.factor(.data[[trait_col]]))
  
  if (n_distinct(dat$group) < 2) return(NULL)
  
  results <- list()
  for (s in unique(dat$site)) {
    for (p in unique(dat$pm_type)) {
      subdat <- dat %>% filter(site == s, pm_type == p)
      if (n_distinct(subdat$group) >= 2) {
        test <- tryCatch({
          rstatix::dunn_test(subdat, as.formula(paste(outcome_col, "~ group")), p.adjust.method = "BH")
        }, error = function(e) NULL)
        if (!is.null(test)) {
          test <- test %>%
            mutate(site = s, pm_type = p, outcome = outcome_name, trait = trait_col)
          results[[paste(s, p)]] <- test
        }
      }
    }
  }
  if (length(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  bind_rows(results)
}

# Compute tables
texture_pair_count <- pairwise_table(combined_sample, "surface_texture", "n_particles", "Particle count")
texture_pair_size <- pairwise_table(combined_sample, "surface_texture", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")
leafsize_pair_count <- pairwise_table(combined_sample, "leaf_size_class", "n_particles", "Particle count")
leafsize_pair_size <- pairwise_table(combined_sample, "leaf_size_class", "mean_particle_area_um2", "Particle size")

# Save tables
if(!is.null(texture_pair_count)) write_csv(texture_pair_count, file.path(tables_dir, "texture_pairwise_count.csv"))
if(!is.null(texture_pair_size)) write_csv(texture_pair_size, file.path(tables_dir, "texture_pairwise_size.csv"))
if(!is.null(leafsize_pair_count)) write_csv(leafsize_pair_count, file.path(tables_dir, "leafsize_pairwise_count.csv"))
if(!is.null(leafsize_pair_size)) write_csv(leafsize_pair_size, file.path(tables_dir, "leafsize_pairwise_size.csv"))

cat("  Pairwise test tables saved.\n")

# ---------------------------
# 13.4 Mixed model diagnostics: residuals vs fitted
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 13.3 Mixed model diagnostic plots ---\n")

# Residuals vs fitted for particle count model
resid_count <- residuals(model_count_full)
fitted_count <- fitted(model_count_full)

p_resid_count <- ggplot(data.frame(fitted = fitted_count, resid = resid_count), 
                        aes(x = fitted, y = resid)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Particle count model: Residuals vs Fitted",
       x = "Fitted values", y = "Residuals") +
  theme_wtp()

# Residuals vs fitted for particle size model
resid_size <- residuals(model_size_full)
fitted_size <- fitted(model_size_full)

p_resid_size <- ggplot(data.frame(fitted = fitted_size, resid = resid_size), 
                       aes(x = fitted, y = resid)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Particle size model: Residuals vs Fitted",
       x = "Fitted values", y = "Residuals") +
  theme_wtp()

save_plot_wtp(p_resid_count, "F24_residuals_fitted_count.png", width = 8, height = 6)
save_plot_wtp(p_resid_size, "F25_residuals_fitted_size.png", width = 8, height = 6)

# ---------------------------
# 13.5 PCA biplot of species performance
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 13.4 PCA biplot of species based on mean particle count and size ---\n")

# Prepare species-level data for PCA
pca_data <- species_performance %>%
  select(site, pm_type, species, mean_particle_count, mean_particle_size) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(site, species), 
              names_from = pm_type, 
              values_from = c(mean_particle_count, mean_particle_size),
              names_sep = "_") %>%
  drop_na()

# Perform PCA
pca_result <- prcomp(pca_data[, -c(1,2)], scale. = TRUE, center = TRUE)
pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x)
pca_scores$site <- pca_data$site
pca_scores$species <- pca_data$species

# Variance explained
var_explained <- round(pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2) * 100, 1)

# Biplot
p_pca <- pca_scores %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = site, label = pretty_species(species))) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = 15) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_site) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA biplot of species based on particle count and size",
    subtitle = paste0("PC1: ", var_explained[1], "%, PC2: ", var_explained[2], "%"),
    x = "Principal Component 1",
    y = "Principal Component 2",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

save_plot_wtp(p_pca, "F26_pca_biplot.png", width = 10, height = 8)

# ---------------------------
# 13.6 Heatmap of species × year particle counts
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 13.5 Heatmap of species × year mean particle count (PM10) ---\n")

heatmap_data <- combined_sample %>%
  filter(pm_type == "PM10") %>%
  group_by(species, year, site) %>%
  summarise(mean_count = mean(n_particles), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species))

p_heatmap <- heatmap_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = species_pretty, fill = mean_count)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#08306B",
                      trans = "log10", na.value = "white",
                      name = "Mean particle count") +
  facet_wrap(~ site, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Species × year heatmap of mean particle count (PM10)",
    x = "Year",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_wtp() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 8),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

save_plot_wtp(p_heatmap, "F27_species_year_heatmap.png", width = 12, height = 10)

# ---------------------------
# 13.7 Map of tree locations with composite scores (using QGIS data)
# ---------------------------
cat("\n--- 13.6 Map of tree locations with composite scores ---\n")

# Load the QGIS export file if it exists
qgis_file <- file.path(tables_dir, "witness_tree_qgis.csv")
if (file.exists(qgis_file)) {
  tree_map_data <- read_csv(qgis_file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    filter(year == "all") %>%  # use all-years summary
    mutate(species_pretty = pretty_species(species_name_full))
  
  # Get composite scores for these species
  composite_scores <- species_scores %>%
    filter(pm_type == "PM10") %>%
    select(site, species, composite) %>%
    distinct()
  
  tree_map_data <- tree_map_data %>%
    left_join(composite_scores, by = c("site", "species_name_full" = "species"))
  
  # Simple map using ggplot2
  p_map <- ggplot(tree_map_data, aes(x = longitude, y = latitude, color = composite)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno", name = "Composite score") +
    facet_wrap(~ site) +
    labs(
      title = "Tree locations coloured by composite performance score (PM10)",
      x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude"
    ) +
    theme_wtp() +
    theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))
  
  save_plot_wtp(p_map, "F28_tree_map_composite.png", width = 10, height = 8)
} else {
  cat("  QGIS file not found. Skipping map figure.\n")
}

cat("\n✅ Section 13 complete. Additional figures and tables saved.\n")
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 14: FINAL EXPORT OF ALL DISSERTATION OUTPUTS
# ============================================================
# Copies all figures and tables to a single organised folder
# for easy submission.
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n              SECTION 14: FINAL EXPORT OF DISSERTATION OUTPUTS                 ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

# Create final dissertation output folder
final_dir <- file.path(outputs_dir, "final_dissertation_outputs")
dir.create(final_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Subfolders
fig_final <- file.path(final_dir, "figures")
tbl_final <- file.path(final_dir, "tables")
dir.create(fig_final, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(tbl_final, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Copy all PNG figures from figures_dir
fig_files <- list.files(figures_dir, pattern = "\\.png$", full.names = FALSE)
for (f in fig_files) {
  file.copy(file.path(figures_dir, f), file.path(fig_final, f), overwrite = TRUE)
}
cat("Copied", length(fig_files), "figures to", fig_final, "\n")

# Copy all CSV tables from tables_dir
tbl_files <- list.files(tables_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = FALSE)
for (t in tbl_files) {
  file.copy(file.path(tables_dir, t), file.path(tbl_final, t), overwrite = TRUE)
}
cat("Copied", length(tbl_files), "tables to", tbl_final, "\n")

# Also copy the R Markdown notebook itself (optional)
# notebook_file <- "Witness_Tree_Clean_Analysis.Rmd"
# if (file.exists(notebook_file)) {
#   file.copy(notebook_file, file.path(final_dir, notebook_file), overwrite = TRUE)
# }

# Create a README file with description
readme_text <- paste(
  "# Witness Tree Project - Final Dissertation Outputs\n",
  "Date:", Sys.Date(), "\n\n",
  "## Figures\n",
  "All figures (F1-F31 and S1-S21) are in the 'figures' folder.\n",
  "Main dissertation figures are labelled F1 to F31.\n",
  "Supplementary figures are labelled S01 to S21.\n\n",
  "## Tables\n",
  "All analysis tables are in the 'tables' folder as CSV files.\n",
  "Key tables: species_performance.csv, summary_by_site_pm.csv, hypothesis_summary.csv, top_recommendations.csv\n\n",
  "## Contact\n",
  "For questions, refer to the original R Markdown notebook.\n"
)
writeLines(readme_text, file.path(final_dir, "README.txt"))

cat("\n✅ Final export complete. All outputs ready in:\n")
cat("  ", final_dir, "\n")
```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 16: QGIS EXPORT WITH COORDINATES (COMBINED DATASET)
# ============================================================
# This section adds coordinates to the combined sample-level data
# and exports a single CSV file suitable for mapping in QGIS.
# ============================================================

cat("\n================================================================================")
cat("\n              SECTION 16: QGIS EXPORT WITH COORDINATES                         ")
cat("\n================================================================================\n")

coords_raw <- tribble(
  ~species_name, ~site, ~latitude, ~longitude,
  "Acacia_melanoxylon", "TCDBG", 53.31250831, -6.259438304,
  "Aesculus_indica", "TCDBG", 53.31184641, -6.260275661,
  "Alnus_glutinosa", "TCDBG", 53.31231879, -6.259385984,
  "Arbutus_unedo", "TCDBG", 53.31183152, -6.259871054,
  "Betula_utilis_subsp_jacquemontil", "TCDBG", 53.31231134, -6.259491343,
  "Carpinus_betulus", "TCDBG", 53.31289558, -6.262574275,
  "Castanea_sativa", "TCDBG", 53.31280597, -6.262592923,
  "Corylus_avellana", "TCDBG", 53.31222129, -6.259479999,
  "Crataegus_monogyna", "TCDBG", 53.31242724, -6.259426595,
  "Davidia_involucrata_var_involucrata", "TCDBG", 53.31232316, -6.260301296,
  "Ginkgo_biloba", "TCDBG", 53.31228767, -6.259717426,
  "Juglans_nigra", "TCDBG", 53.31192616, -6.260197378,
  "Magnolia_grandiflora", "TCDBG", 53.31250261, -6.259663658,
  "Metasequoia_glyptostroboides", "TCDBG", 53.3128954, -6.261328599,
  "Ostrya_carpinifolia", "TCDBG", 53.31300014, -6.261114227,
  "Phoenix_dactylifera", "TCDBG", 53.31284568, -6.26100044,
  "Quercus_robur", "TCDBG", 53.3119908, -6.259699488,
  "Salix_x_fragilis", "TCDBG", 53.312196, -6.259323,
  "Sorbus_hibernica", "TCDBG", 53.31298018, -6.261595302,
  "Tilia_europaea", "TCDBG", 53.31285138, -6.260775085,
  "Wollemia_nobilis", "TCDBG", 53.31286277, -6.260939712,
  "Acacia_melanoxylon", "NBG", 53.37283085, -6.270118887,
  "Aesculus_indica", "NBG", 53.3742823, -6.276833429,
  "Alnus_glutinosa", "NBG", 53.37131408, -6.273575756,
  "Arbutus_unedo", "NBG", 53.37266265, -6.271879088,
  "Betula_utilis_subsp_jacquemontil", "NBG", 53.37340672, -6.276126442,
  "Carpinus_betulus", "NBG", 53.37381031, -6.271124349,
  "Castanea_sativa", "NBG", 53.37265726, -6.273143339,
  "Corylus_avellana", "NBG", 53.37314902, -6.275155491,
  "Crataegus_monogyna", "NBG", 53.37156613, -6.273446939,
  "Davidia_involucrata_var_involucrata", "NBG", 53.37417529, -6.274711371,
  "Ginkgo_biloba", "NBG", 53.37376616, -6.273033471,
  "Magnolia_grandiflora", "NBG", 53.37076453, -6.270614274,
  "Ostrya_carpinifolia", "NBG", 53.37402498, -6.272177698,
  "Phoenix_dactylifera", "NBG", 53.37199492, -6.269414736,
  "Quercus_robur", "NBG", 53.37182773, -6.27451563,
  "Salix_x_fragilis", "NBG", 53.37320304, -6.270661108,
  "Sorbus_hibernica", "NBG", 53.37177244, -6.274068726,
  "Tilia_europaea", "NBG", 53.37463158, -6.275573438,
  "Wollemia_nobilis", "NBG", 53.37333332, -6.271997359
) %>%
  mutate(
    species_name_clean = clean_species_name(species_name),  # optional, but keep for consistency
    site = as.character(site)
  ) %>%
  distinct(species_name, site, .keep_all = TRUE)   # ensure uniqueness

# ---------------------------
# 16.2 Attach coordinates to the combined dataset
# ---------------------------
qgis_data <- combined_sample %>%
  mutate(species_name_clean = clean_species_name(species)) %>%
  left_join(coords_raw, by = c("species_name_clean", "site")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(latitude), !is.na(longitude))

cat("✅ Matched", nrow(qgis_data), "samples with coordinates\n")

# ---------------------------
# 16.3 Create species‑level summary for mapping (one row per species per site)
# ---------------------------
qgis_summary <- qgis_data %>%
  group_by(site, species, species_name_clean, latitude, longitude) %>%
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    n_years = n_distinct(year),
    years_sampled = paste(sort(unique(year)), collapse = ", "),
    mean_particle_count = mean(n_particles, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_particle_count = sd(n_particles, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_particle_size_um2 = mean(mean_particle_area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_particle_size = sd(mean_particle_area_um2, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_particles = sum(n_particles, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Add composite score for PM10 (best overall performance metric)
  left_join(
    species_scores %>% filter(pm_type == "PM10") %>% select(site, species, composite),
    by = c("site", "species")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    composite_score = round(composite, 1),
    species_pretty = pretty_species(species)
  ) %>%
  arrange(site, desc(mean_particle_count))

# ---------------------------
# 16.4 Export the combined QGIS file
# ---------------------------
qgis_export_path <- file.path(final_dir, "qgis_export_all.csv")
write_csv(qgis_summary, qgis_export_path)

cat("\n✅ QGIS export saved to:\n")
cat("  ", qgis_export_path, "\n")
cat("\nColumns included:\n")
cat("  - site, species, latitude, longitude\n")
cat("  - mean_particle_count, sd_particle_count\n")
cat("  - mean_particle_size_um2, sd_particle_size\n")
cat("  - n_samples, n_years, years_sampled\n")
cat("  - composite_score (PM10 composite performance)\n")
cat("  - total_particles\n\n")
cat("You can now open this CSV in QGIS as a delimited text layer.\n")
```
